messages.content:
Question 1: This is what people purchase in order to manage risk. ______________.

 a
Insurance
 b
Car
 c
Credit Card
a. Insurance

Your annual interest with a credit card or loan is known as the_________.

 a
Grace Period

 b
APR

 c
Credit limit
b. APR

In these tax-deferred plans employers sometimes match a portion of employee contributions.______.
 a
Roth IRA
 b
Retirement Plan
 c
401K

This type of financial institution typically charges the lowest interest rate for loans________.
 a
Commercial bank
 b
Credit Union
 c
Life Insurance Company
b. Credit Union

______ is a tax on earnings and is the largest defined benefit plan that exists.

 a
Medicare
 b
Pension
 c
401K
 d
Social Security
d. Social Security

A high school student can have their parent’s ______ if he/she doesn’t not have credit or enough money to fund a loan.

 a
provide for them
 b
cosign
 c
borrow money
b. cosign

Before you start your job you fill out a ______ .  By January 31st you receive a ______  from your employer.  The ______ form is completed by April 15.

BE SURE YOU SELECT THE CORRECT ORDER.

 a
1040EZ, W-2, W-4
 b
W-2, W-4, 1040EZ
 c
W-4, W-2, tax return form

 d
W-4, 1040EZ, W-2

The most popular place in the world to trade stocks is the ____.

 a
Insurance Broker

 b
SEC

 c
Bull Market

 d
NYSE
d. NYSE

A ______  is when investors are pessimistic about the market and a ______  is when investors are optimistic and think it is a great time to buy.

 a
bull/bear
 b
NYSE/Nasdaq
 c
bear/bull
a. bull/bear

The price you pay for an insurance policy.

 a
premium
 b
deductible
 c
claim
a. premium

If you own a home, you need to purchase this type of insurance.

 a
Renters
 b
Homeowners
 c
Health
b. Homeowners

What is the stock symbol for American Express?

 a
NYSE
 b
American Express Co.
 c
AEC
 d
AXP
d. AXP

What is the smallest amount that must be paid by the due date?

 a
$369.75
 b
$10
 c
$507.35
 d
$7

To avoid all finance charges, what amount must be paid by 7/24/10?

 a
$10
 b
$11,000
 c
$849
 d
$507.35

a. If you were to pay only the minimum amount, how much total would it cost to pay off the balance?

b. How long would it take to pay it off?

 a
$849 and 7 years
 b
$18 and 3 years
 c
$845 and 3 years
c. $845 and 3 years

What is this cardholder’s credit limit?

 a
$2,200
 b
$10,492
 c
$11,000

Describe how the financial planning pyramid is organized. Check all that apply!

 a
Low risk v. high risk
 b
risk v. reward
 c
High risk is at the top
 d
Financial security is on the bottom leve

c. High risk is at the top
d. Financial security is on the bottom level
